qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Unauthenticated first connector can hijack the remote validator signer
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: This finding is in the optional `privval` remote-signer trust boundary, while the advisory fixes consensus commit validation and BFT Time derivation from commit signatures. It does not involve the advisory's validator-address mismatch in commit processing.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: RPC indexed search materializes full match sets before pagination
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: This finding is an RPC `tx_search`/`block_search` result-materialization issue, while the advisory is a consensus BFT Time flaw caused by inconsistent commit-signature verification and median-time derivation. There is no shared vulnerable function or equivalent control flow.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory's consensus root cause. The advisory patch hardens commit verification and block-time derivation against inconsistent commit signature validator addresses, while the session's qualifying findings concern remote-signer authentication and RPC search resource usage.
evidence: The advisory summary describes a BFT Time inconsistency between how commit signatures are verified and how block time is derived.

Referenced patch `bf8274fcdbcab2bc652660ae627196a90a6efb97` updates `state/state.go` and `state/validation.go` so `MedianTime` fails when a commit signature's validator address is not in the expected validator set.
The same patch updates `types/validation.go` to reject commit signatures whose `ValidatorAddress` does not match the validator selected during commit verification.
The `privval-unauthenticated-signer-hijack` finding is confined to the `privval` remote signer listener and `SignerClient` trust boundary, not consensus commit validation or block-time derivation.
The `rpc-search-memory-exhaustion` finding is confined to RPC search pagination and index materialization in `/tx_search` and `/block_search`, not BFT Time or commit verification.
